state.title: ../igs-mcp/config/sources.yml

state.output:
<path>/home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/scripts/validate-sources.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
4:  *
5:  * Tests each source URL for:
6:  *   1. HTTP 200 status
7:  *   2. Content-Type header inspection
8:  *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
9:  *
10:  * Usage:
11:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
12:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
13:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
14:  *
15:  * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
16:  *   sources:
17:  *     - url: https://example.com/feed
18:  *       parser: rss
19:  *       name: optional label
20:  *
21:  * Or a flat list:
22:  *   - url: https://example.com/feed
23:  *     parser: rss
24:  *
25:  * Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
26:  */
27: 
28: import RSSParser from 'rss-parser';
29: import { request } from 'undici';
30: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
31: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
32: 
33: const rssParser = new RSSParser({ timeout: 15000 });
34: 
35: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: interface SourceEntry {
38:   url: string;
39:   parser: string;
40:   name?: string;
41: }
42: 
43: interface ValidationResult {
44:   url: string;
45:   parser: string;
46:   status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'partial';
47:   items_count: number;
48:   error?: string;
49:   content_type?: string;
50:   feed_type?: string;
51:   sample_title?: string;
52: }
53: 
54: interface Summary {
55:   total: number;
56:   passed: number;
57:   failed: number;
58:   partial: number;
59: }
60: 
61: interface Report {
62:   summary: Summary;
63:   results: ValidationResult[];
64: }
65: 
66: // ── CLI argument parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67: 
68: function parseArgs(): { entries: SourceEntry[]; filePath: string | null } {
69:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
70:   const entries: SourceEntry[] = [];
71:   let filePath: string | null = null;
72: 
73:   const fileIndex = args.indexOf('--file');
74:   if (fileIndex !== -1 && fileIndex + 1 < args.length) {
75:     filePath = args[fileIndex + 1];
76:   }
77: 
78:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
79:     if (args[i] === '--url' && i + 1 < args.length) {
80:       const url = args[i + 1];
81:       if (!url.startsWith('http://') && !url.startsWith('https://')) {
82:         console.error(`Invalid URL: ${url}`);
83:         process.exit(1);
84:       }
85:       let parser = 'rss';
86:       for (let j = i + 2; j < args.length; j++) {
87:         if (args[j] === '--url' || args[j] === '--file') break;
88:         if (args[j] === '--parser' && j + 1 < args.length) {
89:           parser = args[j + 1];
90:           break;
91:         }
92:       }
93:       entries.push({ url, parser });
94:     }
95:   }
96: 
97:   return { entries, filePath };
98: }
99: 
100: async function loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath: string): Promise<SourceEntry[]> {
101:   const raw = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
102:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
103: 
104:   if (!data) throw new Error(`Empty or invalid YAML file: ${filePath}`);
105: 
106:   const list: any[] = data.sources ?? (Array.isArray(data) ? data : null);
107:   if (!list) throw new Error(`YAML file must have a "sources" key or be a flat list`);
108: 
109:   return list.map((entry: any, idx: number) => {
110:     if (!entry.url) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "url"`);
111:     if (!entry.parser) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "parser"`);
112:     return { url: entry.url, parser: entry.parser, name: entry.name };
113:   });
114: }
115: 
116: // ── Feed type detection helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: function detectFeedType(body: string, contentType: string): string {
119:   const trimmed = body.trim();
120:   if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<rss') || trimmed.startsWith('<feed')) {
121:     if (/<rss[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'rss';
122:     if (/<feed[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'atom';
123:     if (contentType.includes('xml')) return 'xml';
124:     return 'xml';
125:   }
126:   if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
127:     try {
128:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
129:       if (parsed?.version && parsed?.items) return 'json_feed';
130:       if (parsed?.items) return 'json_feed_like';
131:       return 'json';
132:     } catch {
133:       return 'json_like';
134:     }
135:   }
136:   if (trimmed.startsWith('%PDF')) return 'pdf';
137:   return 'html';
138: }
139: 
140: // ── Validation per parser type ───────────────────────────────────────────────
141: 
142: async function validateSource(entry: SourceEntry): Promise<ValidationResult> {
143:   const { url, parser } = entry;
144:   const result: ValidationResult = {
145:     url,
146:     parser,
147:     status: 'fail',
148:     items_count: 0,
149:   };
150: 
151:   let body: string;
152:   let statusCode: number;
153:   let rawContentType: string;
154: 
155:   try {
156:     const controller = new AbortController();
157:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 15000);
158: 
159:     const resp = await request(url, {
160:       method: 'GET',
161:       headers: {
162:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-Validator/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
163:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, application/pdf, */*',
164:       },
165:       signal: controller.signal,
166:     });
167: 
168:     clearTimeout(timeout);
169: 
170:     statusCode = resp.statusCode;
171:     rawContentType = (resp.headers['content-type'] as string) || '';
172:     result.content_type = rawContentType;
173: 
174:     if (statusCode !== 200) {
175:       result.status = 'fail';
176:       result.error = `HTTP ${statusCode}`;
177:       return result;
178:     }
179: 
180:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
181:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
182:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
183:     }
184:     body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
185: 
186:     if (body.length === 0) {
187:       result.status = 'fail';
188:       result.error = 'Empty response body';
189:       return result;
190:     }
191:   } catch (err: any) {
192:     result.status = 'fail';
193:     if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
194:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
195:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
196:       result.error = `DNS resolution failed: ${err.message}`;
197:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
198:       result.error = `Connection refused: ${err.message}`;
199:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
200:       result.error = `Connection reset: ${err.message}`;
201:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
202:       result.error = 'Headers timeout';
203:     } else if (err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
204:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
205:     } else {
206:       result.error = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
207:     }
208:     return result;
209:   }
210: 
211:   result.feed_type = detectFeedType(body, rawContentType);
212: 
213:   try {
214:     await simulateParse(parser, body, result);
215:   } catch (parseErr: any) {
216:     result.status = 'fail';
217:     result.error = `Parse error: ${parseErr.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(parseErr)}`;
218:   }
219: 
220:   return result;
221: }
222: 
223: async function simulateParse(
224:   parser: string,
225:   body: string,
226:   result: ValidationResult,
227: ): Promise<void> {
228:   switch (parser) {
229:     case 'rss': {
230:       let feed;
231:       try {
232:         feed = await rssParser.parseString(body);
233:       } catch {
234:         // rss-parser may also handle Atom; if it fails, try as raw XML
235:         feed = { items: [] };
236:       }
237:       const items = feed.items || [];
238:       result.items_count = items.length;
239: 
240:       if (items.length > 0) {
241:         result.status = 'ok';
242:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title?.slice(0, 120);
243:       } else {
244:         // Check if body at least looks like valid RSS/XML
245:         if (body.includes('<item>') || body.includes('<entry>') || body.includes('<rss') || body.includes('<feed')) {
246:           result.status = 'partial';
247:           result.error = 'Feed parsed but 0 items';
248:         } else {
249:           result.status = 'fail';
250:           result.error = 'Content does not appear to be RSS/Atom XML';
251:         }
252:       }
253:       break;
254:     }
255: 
256:     case 'json_feed': {
257:       let data: any;
258:       try {
259:         data = JSON.parse(body);
260:       } catch {
261:         result.status = 'fail';
262:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON';
263:         return;
264:       }
265: 
266:       const items = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items
267:         : Array.isArray(data) ? data
268:         : Array.isArray(data?.value) ? data.value
269:         : [];
270: 
271:       result.items_count = items.length;
272: 
273:       const hasVersion = typeof data?.version === 'string' || data?.version !== undefined;
274:       const hasTitle = typeof data?.title === 'string';
275: 
276:       if (items.length > 0) {
277:         result.status = 'ok';
278:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title || items[0]?.url || '(no title)';
279:         result.sample_title = String(result.sample_title).slice(0, 120);
280:       } else if (hasVersion || hasTitle) {
281:         result.status = 'partial';
282:         result.error = 'JSON feed structure recognized but 0 items';
283:       } else if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length > 0) {
284:         // Could be an array of items (some APIs return this)
285:         result.status = 'ok';
286:         result.items_count = data.length;
287:         result.sample_title = String(data[0]?.title || data[0]?.url || '').slice(0, 120);
288:       } else {
289:         result.status = 'fail';
290:         result.error = 'JSON does not match feed structure (missing version/items)';
291:       }
292:       break;
293:     }
294: 
295:     case 'generic_html':
296:     case 'article_content': {
297:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
298:       if (stripped.length > 0) {
299:         result.status = 'partial';
300:         result.items_count = 0;
301:         result.feed_type = result.feed_type || 'html';
302:       } else {
303:         result.status = 'fail';
304:         result.error = 'Empty or whitespace-only HTML body';
305:       }
306:       break;
307:     }
308: 
309:     case 'sitemap': {
310:       const trimmed = body.trim();
311:       if (trimmed.includes('<urlset') || trimmed.includes('<sitemapindex')) {
312:         const locMatches = body.match(/<loc>/g);
313:         result.items_count = locMatches ? locMatches.length : 0;
314:         result.status = result.items_count > 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial';
315:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'Sitemap parsed but 0 URLs found';
316:       } else if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<')) {
317:         result.status = 'partial';
318:         result.items_count = 0;
319:         result.error = 'XML content but not a recognized sitemap format';
320:       } else {
321:         result.status = 'fail';
322:         result.error = 'Not a sitemap XML';
323:       }
324:       break;
325:     }
326: 
327:     case 'ofac':
328:     case 'ussf_cfc':
329:     case 'newslaundry': {
330:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
331:       if (stripped.length > 100) {
332:         result.status = 'partial';
333:         result.items_count = 0;
334:       } else {
335:         result.status = 'fail';
336:         result.error = `Body too short for ${parser} parser (${stripped.length} chars text)`;
337:       }
338:       break;
339:     }
340: 
341:     case 'who_dons':
342:     case 'semantic_scholar': {
343:       try {
344:         const data = JSON.parse(body);
345:         result.status = 'partial';
346:         result.items_count = Array.isArray(data) ? data.length
347:           : Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items.length
348:           : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results.length
349:           : 0;
350:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'JSON parsed but 0 items found in expected fields';
351:       } catch {
352:         result.status = 'fail';
353:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON for parser type';
354:       }
355:       break;
356:     }
357: 
358:     case 'pdf-extractor':
359:     case 'pdf-to-markdown': {
360:       if (body.startsWith('%PDF') || body.charCodeAt(0) === 0x25) {
361:         result.status = 'partial';
362:         result.items_count = 0;
363:         result.feed_type = 'pdf';
364:       } else {
365:         result.status = 'fail';
366:         result.error = 'Not a PDF file (missing %PDF header)';
367:       }
368:       break;
369:     }
370: 
371:     default: {
372:       result.status = 'partial';
373:       result.items_count = 0;
374:       result.error = `Unknown parser type "${parser}" — only connectivity verified`;
375:       break;
376:     }
377:   }
378: }
379: 
380: // ── Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
381: 
382: function printReport(results: ValidationResult[]): void {
383:   const summary: Summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, partial: 0 };
384: 
385:   for (const r of results) {
386:     summary.total++;
387:     if (r.status === 'ok') summary.passed++;
388:     else if (r.status === 'fail') summary.failed++;
389:     else if (r.status === 'partial') summary.partial++;
390:   }
391: 
392:   const report: Report = { summary, results };
393:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
394: }
395: 
396: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
397: 
398: async function main() {
399:   const { entries: cliEntries, filePath } = parseArgs();
400:   let allEntries: SourceEntry[] = [...cliEntries];
401: 
402:   if (filePath) {
403:     try {
404:       const fileEntries = await loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath);
405:       allEntries = allEntries.concat(fileEntries);
406:     } catch (err: any) {
407:       console.error(`Error loading YAML file "${filePath}": ${err.message}`);
408:       process.exit(1);
409:     }
410:   }
411: 
412:   if (allEntries.length === 0) {
413:     console.error('No sources to validate. Provide --url/--parser pairs or --file.');
414:     console.error('');
415:     console.error('Usage:');
416:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>');
417:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>');
418:     process.exit(1);
419:   }
420: 
421:   const results: ValidationResult[] = [];
422: 
423:   for (let i = 0; i < allEntries.length; i++) {
424:     const entry = allEntries[i];
425:     const label = entry.name || entry.url;
426:     process.stderr.write(`[${i + 1}/${allEntries.length}] ${label}... `);
427: 
428:     const result = await validateSource(entry);
429:     results.push(result);
430: 
431:     const icons: Record<string, string> = { ok: '✓', fail: '✗', partial: '~' };
432:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[result.status] || '?'} ${result.status}`);
433:     if (result.items_count > 0) process.stderr.write(` (${result.items_count} items)`);
434:     if (result.error) process.stderr.write(` — ${result.error.slice(0, 80)}`);
435:     process.stderr.write('\n');
436:   }
437: 
438:   printReport(results);
439: 
440:   const anyFailed = results.some(r => r.status === 'fail');
441:   process.exit(anyFailed ? 1 : 0);
442: }
443: 
444: main().catch(err => {
445:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
446:   process.exit(1);
447: });

(End of file - total 447 lines)
</content>

#!/usr/bin/env bun
/**
 * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
 *
 * Tests each source URL for:
 *   1. HTTP 200 status
 *   2. Content-Type header inspection
 *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
 *
 * Usage:
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
 *
 * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
 *   sources:
 *     - url: https://example.com/feed
 *       parser: rss
 *       name: optional label
 *

[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/README.md]
# IGS MCP Server

**Intelligence Gathering System** — Local-only MCP server for real-time news monitoring and academic research.

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/igs-mcp-server)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/igs-mcp-server)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)](https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp/blob/main/LICENSE)

---

## 🎯 What It Does

IGS is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that provides AI assistants with:

1. **News Monitoring** — Fetch from 223 curated RSS/HTTP sources (Reuters, BBC, Indian Express, etc.)
2. **Reddit Search** — Search any subreddit with time/sort filters
3. **Academic Research** — Search arXiv + Semantic Scholar papers with PDF extraction
4. **Historical Queries** — Access news from specific date ranges via Google News

All processing happens **locally on your machine**. No data is sent to external servers except the news sources themselves.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### 1. Install & Configure (Recommended)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
./setup.sh
```

The setup script checks prerequisites (Node.js v20+), installs dependencies, builds, bootstraps config files, and prints your Claude Desktop config at the end — ready to copy-paste.

### Manual Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
npm install
npm run build
```

### 2. Configure Claude Desktop

Edit `claude_desktop_config.json`:

**macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Linux:** `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "igs": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/igs-mcp/dist/server.js"]
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. Restart Claude Desktop

Completely quit and relaunch Claude Desktop.

---

## 📚 Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [INSTALL.md](INSTALL.md) | Complete installation guide |
| [AUDIT_REPORT.md](AUDIT_REPORT.md) | Installation audit & verification |
| `docs/plans/` | Architecture & design documents |

---

## 🛠️ Available Tools

### News Monitoring

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `news.fetch` | Fetch from curated sources | `news.fetch(pools=['GLOBAL_BREAKING'], keywords=['AI'], limit=10)` |
| `news.testSource` | Test single source | `news.testSource(id='reuters')` |

### Research Tools

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `reddit.search` | Search Reddit | `reddit.search(query='technology', subreddits=['news'], limit=10)` |
| `research.search` | Search papers | `research.search(query='transformer', sources=['arxiv'], limit=10)` |
| `research.paper` | Paper details + PDF | `research.paper(paperId='arxiv:2401.12345', extractPDF=true)` |

### Source Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `sources.list` | List all sources |
| `sources.upsert` | Add/update source |
| `sources.delete` | Remove source |
| `sources.countries` | Countries with source counts |
| `sources.cities` | Cities with source counts |
| `sources.domains` | Topic domains |

### Pool Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pools.list` | List all pools |
| `pools.upsert` | Create/update pool |
| `pools.delete` | Delete pool |

---

## 📊 Pre-configured Content

### News Pools (14)

| Pool | Description | Sources |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `GLOBAL_BREAKING` | Fast breaking news | Reuters, BBC, FT, Al Jazeera |
| `GLOBAL_GEOECON` | Geopolitics & geoeconomics | Foreign Affairs, RAND, Brookings |
| `GLOBAL_LAW_REG` | Law & regulation | OFAC, Lawfare, SCOTUSblog |
| `GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER` | Tech & cyber | MIT Tech Review, Ars Technica, CISA |
| `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` | Environment & health | WHO, Mongabay, Nature |
| `GLOBAL_CULT_SOC` | Culture & society | Aeon, UnHerd, Noema |
| `INDIA_NATIONAL_BASE` | India national | The Hindu, Indian Express, Economic Times |
| `INDIA_WATCHDOG` | India investigative | Scroll.in, The Wire, Article 14 |
| `INDIA_FACTCHECK_DATA` | India fact-check | Alt News, BOOM Live, IndiaSpend |
| `INDIA_BUSINESS_REG` | India business | Moneycontrol, NSE, RBI, SEBI |
| `INDIA_REGION` | India regional | IE South/East/Northeast |
| `INDIA_CITIES` | India cities | Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru feeds |
| `GLOBAL_COUNTRIES` | Country feeds | Guardian/France24 country feeds |
| `GLOBAL_CITIES` | Global cities | London, NYC, Singapore |

### Source Inventory

- **Total Sources:** 223
- **Countries Covered:** 45
- **Cities Covered:** 23
- **Topic Domains:** 23 (geopolitics, finance, tech, science, etc.)

---

## 🔧 Configuration

### User Config Directory

IGS stores configuration in:

- **Linux/macOS:** `~/.config/igs-mcp/`
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\igs-mcp\`
- **Custom:** Set `IGS_CONFIG_DIR` environment variable

### Files

```
~/.config/igs-mcp/
├── pools.yml      # Pool definitions
├── sources.yml    # News sources (223 configured)
└── settings.yml   # HTTP/cache settings
```

### Add Custom Source

Edit `~/.config/igs-mcp/sources.yml`:

```yaml
- id: my_tech_blog
  name: My Tech Blog
  type: rss
  url: https://example.com/feed.xml
  parser: rss
  pools: [GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER]
  countries: [US]
  domains: [tech]
  is_active: true
```

No restart required — changes load automatically.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           MCP Client (Claude)               │
└─────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                  │ stdio
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────┐
│         IGS MCP Server (Node.js)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Tools: 11 MCP tools                        │
│  • news.fetch      • research.search        │
│  • reddit.search   • research.paper         │
│  • sources.*       • pools.*                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Parsers: 9 parsers                         │
│  • RSS/Atom        • Generic HTML           │
│  • Semantic Scholar• PDF extraction         │
│  • OFAC, WHO, USSF (custom HTML)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  APIs:                                      │
│  • Reddit Search   • arXiv OAI-PMH          │
│  • Semantic Scholar• Google News proxy      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## ⚡ Performance

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Cold start | ~2 seconds |
| news.fetch (9 sources) | ~3 seconds |
| news.fetch (223 sources) | ~25 seconds |
| Cache hit ratio | ~90% (warm) |
| Memory usage | 50-150 MB |

---

## 🔒 Security

- ✅ **Local-only** — Runs on your machine
- ✅ **No telemetry** — No data collection
- ✅ **HTTPS only** — All external requests encrypted
- ✅ **No API keys required** — For basic functionality
- ✅ **User-controlled config** — You own your data

---

## 📦 Dependencies

### Runtime (15)

- @modelcontextprotocol/sdk — MCP protocol
- cheerio — HTML parsing
- pdf-parse — PDF text extraction
- xml2js — arXiv XML parsing
- p-queue — Concurrency control
- undici — HTTP client
- zod — Schema validation
- js-yaml — YAML parsing
- rss-parser — RSS/Atom parsing
- pino — Logging
- compromise — NLP (enrichment)
- sentiment — Sentiment analysis
- keyword-extractor — Keyword extraction
- date-fns + date-fns-tz — Date handling

### Development (5)

- typescript
- @types/node
- @types/js-yaml
- @types/xml2js
- ts-node-dev

---

## 🧪 Development

```bash
# Install
npm install

# Development mode (auto-reload)
npm run dev

# Build
npm run build

# Type check
npm run typecheck

# Start production
npm start
```

---

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Server won't start

```bash
# Check Node version (must be v20+)
node --version

# Rebuild
rm -rf dist/
npm run build
```

### Config not loading

```bash
# Check user config directory
ls -la ~/.config/igs-mcp/

# Force bootstrap
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/
npm start
```

### Cache issues

```bash
# Clear all caches
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/cache/
rm -rf ./cache/
```

### Rate limiting

- **Semantic Scholar:** 100 requests/day (free tier)
- **Reddit:** ~60 requests/minute
- **arXiv:** 3000 requests/day

Tool includes automatic retry with backoff.

---

## 📝 Changelog

### v0.2.0 (April 2026)

**Major Upgrade: Research Tools**

- ✨ NEW: `reddit.search` — Dynamic Reddit search
- ✨ NEW: `research.search` — arXiv + Semantic Scholar
- ✨ NEW: `research.paper` — PDF extraction with citations
- 🔧 Parallel source fetching (6 concurrent)
- 🔧 Historical date queries via Google News URL rewriting
- 🗑️ REMOVED: RSS-based Reddit/arXiv/Semantic Scholar sources
- 📚 Added INSTALL.md and AUDIT_REPORT.md
- 🛠️ Added `setup.sh` one-line installer with auto-config

### v0.1.0 (Initial Release)

- Core news fetching with pools/sources
- RSS/HTTP parsers
- YAML configuration
- Local caching with ETag support

---

## Task 19: Health Monitor Script

- Created `scripts/health-monitor.ts` — standalone health check for all 366 sources
- Uses `undici` for HTTP requests and `js-yaml` for YAML parsing (no imports from `src/`)
- Health check logic:
  - **RSS/HTTP**: HTTP GET with 10s header timeout + 5s body timeout. <3s = healthy, 3-10s = stale, errors = failing
  - **social_media**: Validates platform (reddit/twitter), checks `rate_limit` config completeness
  - **Inactive**: `is_active: false` → marked inactive, excluded from total count
- Run: `bun run scripts/health-monitor.ts`
- Exit code: 0 if all healthy, 1 if any failures
- Output: JSON to stdout, progress to stderr
- First run results: 358 active sources → 287 healthy, 67 failing, 4 stale, 8 inactive
- Config resolution: checks `IGS_CONFIG_DIR` env var, defaults to `config/sources.yml`
- Many "failing" sources are HTTP 301/302 redirects (expected for news sites behind redirects)
- Google News-based sources returning 302 are likely blocked — may need UA headers updated
EOF
